concepts.name: Inductive Bias
concepts.description: A structural assumption encoded into a neural architecture to restrict its hypothesis space and improve learning efficiency for specific data types.

concepts.name: Arithmetic Intensity
concepts.description: The ratio of floating-point operations performed per byte of data moved from main memory, determining hardware bottlenecks.

concepts.name: Compute-Bound Workload
concepts.description: A processing regime where execution time is strictly limited by the hardware's arithmetic throughput rather than data transfer rates.

concepts.name: Bandwidth-Bound Workload
concepts.description: A processing regime where execution time is limited by the speed of transferring data between memory and compute units.

concepts.name: Memory-Capacity-Bound Workload
concepts.description: A system state where execution is limited by the raw physical storage required for massive parameter tables, typical of recommendation systems.

concepts.name: Learnability Gap
concepts.description: The practical distinction between what a network can theoretically represent versus what it can successfully optimize given finite data and compute.

concepts.name: Dense Matrix Multiplication
concepts.description: The foundational all-to-all computational pattern of MLPs that utilizes structured parallel multiply-accumulate hardware units.

concepts.name: Translation Equivariance
concepts.description: An architectural property achieved via parameter sharing where shifting the input produces a structurally corresponding shift in the output feature map.

concepts.name: im2col Transformation
concepts.description: A hardware-level memory layout optimization that rearranges overlapping sliding-window image patches into columns to execute convolutions as efficient matrix multiplications.

concepts.name: Depthwise Separable Convolution
concepts.description: An architectural factorization that splits standard convolution into independent spatial and channel-wise operations to drastically reduce FLOPs for resource-constrained edge systems.

concepts.name: Sequential Dependency
concepts.description: The inherent hardware constraint in recurrent networks where time step computations cannot be parallelized because each step waits on the previous step's hidden state.

concepts.name: Dynamic Attention Routing
concepts.description: A mechanism that computes mathematical relevance between input elements via queries and keys to generate data-dependent execution pathways.

concepts.name: Quadratic Attention Bottleneck
concepts.description: The severe computational and memory constraint during Transformer training where materializing the attention matrix scales operationally at O(N^2) with sequence length.

concepts.name: Autoregressive KV Cache
concepts.description: An inference memory buffer that stores previously computed key and value vectors to avoid redundant computation, shifting the system bottleneck entirely to memory bandwidth.

concepts.name: Sparse Embedding Lookups
concepts.description: Index-based memory operations in recommendation models that retrieve dense vectors for categorical features, creating random memory access patterns with zero arithmetic intensity.

concepts.name: Embedding Table Sharding
concepts.description: A distributed model parallelism strategy that partitions massive, memory-capacity-bound recommendation tables across multiple GPUs to overcome single-node limits.

concepts.name: All-to-All Communication
concepts.description: A bottlenecking network communication topology in distributed recommendation inference where every GPU node must continuously exchange data with all other nodes.

concepts.name: Skip Connections
concepts.description: Architectural bypass pathways that sum outputs across multiple layers to allow gradients to flow unimpeded, directly solving the vanishing gradient problem in deep networks.
